title: Zaluzianskia macropoda

The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of digestive issues and joint pain. The bioactive constituents of Zaluzianskia macropoda include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and essential oils, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Zaluzianskia macropoda is commonly prepared as a tea or a tincture to take advantage of its potential health benefits, although more research is needed to fully understand its efficacy and safety.

It belongs to the genus Zaluzianskia, a group of plants native to Africa. The specific epithet 'macropoda' refers to the plant's large leaves. The plant is further classified in the tribe Anthemideae, which is a subgroup within the Asteraceae family.
